key_insight: Theoria verifies AI answers by rewriting them into auditable typed state transitions, so any unsupported change is exposed as an explicit unlicensed mutation.
engineering_relevance: This makes high-coverage LLM judging more trustworthy for deployment in theorem proving and expert QA by adding post-hoc auditability without relying on full formal proofs.
method: Rewrite candidate answers into a sequence of typed proof-state transitions.
Require each transition to be justified by a citation, computation, or problem-given fact.
Enforce completeness of change so every state difference must be accounted for.
evidence_signal: On HLE-Verified Gold, Theoria certifies 105/185 problems at 91.4% strict precision, reaches 97.1% certified precision on GPQA Diamond, and catches 94.7% of adversarial poisoned proofs versus 83.2% for holistic judging.
watch_item: The approach depends on high-quality typed rewrites and explicit justifications, so weak parsing, incomplete evidence, or ambiguous state representations could reduce coverage or introduce verifier bottlenecks.
paper_title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
